context.approach: User-uploaded screenshots reviewed by the multimodal GTM synthesis layer against the same seven UX/UI audit axes.

methodology.description: Screenshots are uploaded directly by the user and preserved as report evidence.
A multimodal model reviews visible UI evidence against seven GTM UX/UI axes.
The highest-impact issues are translated into sales-facing recommendations and a static report.

profile.visual_review_goal: Evaluate uploaded UI screenshots against the same seven GTM UX/UI audit axes used by the website audit mode.

scannedPages.title: 002 017 c042 contact

result.site_summary: The site delivers essential legal and contact information with consistent branding but lacks authentic visual elements and social proof critical for B2B trust-building. Navigation is clear but key GTM elements like client validation are missing.

task_execution.observation: Contact page clearly presents multiple contact methods (address, email, phone, calendar) with minimal friction; legal page is purely informational with no actionable tasks.

flow_architecture.observation: Navigation is consistent across pages with logical footer access to legal page; contact page is directly accessible via main navigation without confusing flows.

trust_accessibility.observation: Legal page provides comprehensive company details (RCS, SIRET, address) building regulatory trust; contact page offers verified channels but lacks social proof elements that would enhance credibility for B2B buyers.

ui_consistency.observation: Header/footer and color scheme are consistent; content card layouts differ slightly between pages (2x2 grid vs 3-column) but maintain cohesive styling and spacing.

visual_brand.observation: Dark green brand color is consistently applied, but contact page uses generic blurred office background imagery that fails to convey company authenticity or unique value.

content_microcopy.observation: Contact page labels are clear but contains informal microcopy error ('Calendar google' instead of 'Calendrier Google'); legal page uses precise regulatory terminology.

market_alignment.observation: Contact page effectively drives engagement with clear CTAs but misses critical B2B trust elements like client logos or testimonials; legal page serves compliance but doesn't support GTM storytelling.

priority_issues.title: Contact page lacks B2B social proof elements
priority_issues.reason: B2B buyers require validation through client logos/testimonials to reduce perceived risk; absence on primary conversion page weakens credibility and conversion potential.
priority_issues.evidence: Contact page (Screenshot 2) presents only contact methods without any proof of credibility (e.g., client logos, case studies, or testimonials).
priority_issues.recommendation: Add a section with 3-4 client logos or a short testimonial quote above contact methods to build trust before engagement.

priority_issues.title: Generic stock imagery reduces authenticity perception
priority_issues.reason: B2B buyers expect authentic company representation; generic office background appears impersonal and fails to differentiate the brand.
priority_issues.evidence: Contact page (Screenshot 2) uses blurred, non-unique office background that lacks company-specific elements or human presence.
priority_issues.recommendation: Replace with custom photo of actual office space or team members (with consent) to enhance authenticity and trust.

criteria_discoveries.evidence: Link labeled 'Calendar google' instead of standard French term 'Calendrier Google'
criteria_discoveries.why_it_matters: Undermines brand professionalism in French market; inconsistent terminology creates subtle credibility gaps for local buyers.
criteria_discoveries.recommendation: Use 'Calendrier Google' to align with French language standards and maintain brand polish.

visual_trust_findings.title: Generic stock office background on contact page
visual_trust_findings.evidence: Blurred office background image (Screenshot 2) shows generic shelves/desk without unique company elements or human presence
visual_trust_findings.why_it_matters: B2B buyers seek authentic company representation; generic imagery reduces perceived credibility and fails to differentiate from competitors using similar stock assets
visual_trust_findings.recommendation: Replace with custom photography showing actual workspace or team to build genuine connection

strengths: Consistent navigation and branding across all pages
Comprehensive legal information with clear regulatory details
Multiple verified contact channels presented with minimal friction
result.market_positioning: Positions as a professional B2B service provider through clear contact pathways and regulatory compliance, but fails to establish competitive differentiation or social proof needed to convert skeptical enterprise buyers.

aiDiscoveredFindings.title: Contact page lacks B2B social proof elements
aiDiscoveredFindings.evidence: Contact page (Screenshot 2) presents only contact methods without any proof of credibility (e.g., client logos, case studies, or testimonials).
aiDiscoveredFindings.explanation: On Contact, the review found that 'Contact page lacks B2B social proof elements' may not be fully supported. B2B buyers require validation through client logos/testimonials to reduce perceived risk; absence on primary conversion page weakens credibility and conversion potential.
aiDiscoveredFindings.whyItMatters: This matters because prospects may not immediately understand why this product is relevant for their context or market. In a GTM context, visible friction in Contact can reduce clarity, trust, or conversion readiness.
aiDiscoveredFindings.recommendation: Add a section with 3-4 client logos or a short testimonial quote above contact methods to build trust before engagement.

aiDiscoveredFindings.title: Generic stock imagery reduces authenticity perception
aiDiscoveredFindings.evidence: Contact page (Screenshot 2) uses blurred, non-unique office background that lacks company-specific elements or human presence.
aiDiscoveredFindings.explanation: On Contact, the review found that 'Generic stock imagery reduces authenticity perception' may not be fully supported. B2B buyers expect authentic company representation; generic office background appears impersonal and fails to differentiate the brand.
aiDiscoveredFindings.whyItMatters: This matters because the interface does not project enough confidence, polish, or brand distinctiveness at first glance. In a GTM context, visible friction in Contact can reduce clarity, trust, or conversion readiness.
aiDiscoveredFindings.recommendation: Replace with custom photo of actual office space or team members (with consent) to enhance authenticity and trust.

aiDiscoveredFindings.evidence: Link labeled 'Calendar google' instead of standard French term 'Calendrier Google'

axes.name: Task Execution
axes.description: How clearly the product helps users complete key actions without friction.
axes.summary: Contact page clearly presents multiple contact methods (address, email, phone, calendar) with minimal friction; legal page is purely informational with no actionable tasks.
axes.businessImpact: Friction in key tasks increases drop-off and weakens the product story in a live sales context.

strengths.recommendation: Preserve this strength while addressing higher-priority friction.

painPoints.title: Generic stock imagery reduces authenticity perception
painPoints.evidence: Contact page (Screenshot 2) uses blurred, non-unique office background that lacks company-specific elements or human presence.
painPoints.explanation: On Contact, the review found that 'Generic stock imagery reduces authenticity perception' may not be fully supported. B2B buyers expect authentic company representation; generic office background appears impersonal and fails to differentiate the brand.
painPoints.whyItMatters: This matters because the interface does not project enough confidence, polish, or brand distinctiveness at first glance. In a GTM context, visible friction in Contact can reduce clarity, trust, or conversion readiness.
painPoints.recommendation: Replace with custom photo of actual office space or team members (with consent) to enhance authenticity and trust.

axes.name: Market Alignment
axes.description: How well the offer, audience cues, proof points, and CTA strategy support a GTM story.
axes.summary: Contact page effectively drives engagement with clear CTAs but misses critical B2B trust elements like client logos or testimonials; legal page serves compliance but doesn't support GTM storytelling.
axes.businessImpact: Weak GTM alignment makes it harder for prospects to see why this product fits them now.
